claims.evidence: Graph found 8 companies in Digital Payments: Stripe ($8.8B raised), Checkout.com ($1.8B), Ramp ($1.6B), Brex ($1.2B), Plaid ($734M), Square ($590M), Adyen ($266M), Mercury ($163M)

memo: Deal memo for Acme Payments, Series A. Verdict: Further Diligence recommended, scoring 7.2 out of 10. The strongest signal is the team — CEO Jane Chen previously led payments infrastructure at Stripe for four years, and CTO Marcus Rivera built the ML platform at Scale AI. The $50B TAM claim in digital payments checks out against industry data showing 15.2% annual growth. However, Acme claims no direct competitors, but our analysis found 8 funded companies in the same market, including Stripe at $8.8B raised and Checkout.com at $1.8B. Traction claims of 3x YoY growth and 500 enterprise customers could not be independently verified. Bottom line: strong team, real market, but competitive pressure is significantly higher than the deck suggests. Recommend deeper diligence on defensibility and unit economics before proceeding.
